tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Messi's Words of Grace: Argentina's Star Honors Cape Verde's Heroic Goalkeeper
stories.summary: After an epic match, Lionel Messi embraces Cape Verde's 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha, offering words of praise that will stay with him forever.

stories.headline: Toddlers with Sickle Cell Now Offered a Cure—Gene Therapy Opens New Hope
stories.summary: Children as young as two can now access CRISPR gene therapy, potentially curing sickle cell disease before irreversible organ damage occurs.

stories.headline: Twenty Years of Patriotic Love: One Woman's Flag Tradition Unites Her Entire Neighborhood
stories.summary: Ruth Perdue's 20-year tradition places 144 flags at every mailbox on her street each Fourth of July, bringing neighbors together and delighting her elderly friends.

stories.headline: Tangled and Trapped: Humpback Whale Gets a Second Chance Thanks to Concerned Mariners
stories.summary: Concerned fishermen spotted a juvenile humpback whale tangled in two 800-pound crab pots in Alaska's Endicott Arm and alerted rescuers, who freed the animal to swim again.

stories.headline: Young Filipino Star Alex Eala Stuns Wimbledon Champion in Career-Defining Upset
stories.summary: Twenty-one-year-old Filipino phenom Alex Eala defeats defending champion Iga Swiatek 6-4, 3-6, 7-5 on Centre Court in a career-defining Wimbledon third-round upset.

stories.headline: Depression Breakthrough: Nerve Stimulation Frees One in Five from Treatment-Resistant Despair
stories.summary: A surgically implanted vagus nerve stimulation device left one in five people with severe treatment-resistant depression symptom-free after two years.

stories.headline: Kindness Served: Restaurant Owner Goes Extra Mile for Fasting Customer
stories.summary: A Bangalore restaurant owner who couldn't deliver through the app instead sent a fasting customer double portions of sabudana khichdi with curd and fruit after a 30-minute call.

stories.headline: Scientists Build First Fully Synthetic Cell From Scratch—It Feeds, Grows, and Divides
stories.summary: University of Minnesota researchers created SpudCell, a chemically-defined synthetic organism that lives and replicates, opening doors to custom medicines and bio-engineered materials.

stories.headline: Maine's Piping Plovers Soar to Record Numbers—186 Breeding Pairs Signal Thriving Coasts
stories.summary: Maine celebrates a record 186 piping plover breeding pairs this year, a dozen more than the previous record, signaling thriving and restored coastal ecosystems.

stories.headline: Experimental Drug Halts the Spread of Nerve Damage in ALS Patients
stories.summary: Researchers engineered XL20, an experimental drug that crosses the blood-brain barrier to block toxic TDP-43 proteins and halt nerve cell death in ALS.

stories.headline: Tiny Wings, Mighty Mission: Over 1,300 Oregon Silverspot Butterflies Released Into the Wild
stories.summary: Oregon releases 1,300 rare native butterfly caterpillars into protected coastal habitats as a decades-long restoration mission enters another promising season.
